message:
chore(beads): close bd-qdsc6 bd-vitcn bd-hzkc6 signalfd4 flag/update pins

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-he3h","title":"sysvipc: msgrcv NULL payload with invalid queue id should return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for msgrcv(-1, NULL, 8, 0, 0), so the invalid queue-id path wins over the NULL payload in this case. Add an exact ABI regression test for that precedence rule.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:26:20.439700323Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:17:06.906823158Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:16:36.893118430Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-i5fto","title":"syscall: sched_get_priority_min negative policy should return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for sched_get_priority_min(-1). Add focused ABI regression coverage so the negative-policy contract is explicit apart from the existing large-invalid-policy case.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:33:48.534935980Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:42:34.512419525Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:42:34.510363683Z","close_reason":"Added sched_get_priority_min_negative_policy_is_einval asserting EINVAL for policy=-1.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ibh7","title":"Divergent ABI source scanning between build.rs and check_replacement_guard.sh breaks standalone build","description":"`crates/frankenlibc-abi/build.rs` scans all `.rs` files in `src/` for `libc::` callthroughs, which catches `host_resolve.rs:51 libc::dlvsym` and breaks the `standalone` build. However, `scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh` only scans `*_abi.rs` files, missing `host_resolve.rs` entirely. Furthermore, `build.rs` does not implement the newly added `source_scan_exceptions` from `replacement_profile.json`.\n\nTo fix:\n1. Make `check_replacement_guard.sh` scan all `.rs` files (or align the scopes).\n2. Update `build.rs` to respect `source_scan_exceptions` so `host_resolve::dlvsym` (and any future exceptions) can be whitelisted.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T19:12:58.484175575Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T20:33:37.318380234Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T20:33:37.316445662Z","close_reason":"No repro; build.rs and replacement guard already align on *_abi.rs scanning and source_scan_exceptions","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-rth1","title":"bd-yos subtask: TLS handoff and teardown correctness for spawned threads","description":"Background:
- TLS handoff correctness is required for thread-local errno and future pthread_key destructor behavior.

Goal:
- Implement and verify TLS initialization/teardown handoff for spawned threads.

Deliverables:
1) TLS init protocol during thread start.
2) Per-thread errno/TLS storage linkage checks.
3) Teardown sequencing and cleanup invariants.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Thread-local values remain isolated and stable under concurrent workloads.
- TLS teardown leaves no leaked state.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for TLS setup/teardown and isolation.
- E2E concurrent tests validating cross-thread isolation.
- Structured logs with trace_id, thread_id, tls_event, key_count, cleanup_status.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:52.128037997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T06:12:33.353261867Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T06:12:33.353242521Z","close_reason":"Completed TLS handoff/teardown correctness fixes and verification evidence posted in comments.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","implementation","pthread","testing","tls"],"comments":[{"id":1142,"issue_id":"bd-rth1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Codex claiming this now. Found a concrete TLS table correctness bug: removing one TID currently breaks linear-probe lookup chains for colliding TIDs, which can corrupt per-thread TLS lookup after teardown. I am implementing tombstone-based delete + collision tests now.","created_at":"2026-02-22T05:19:32Z"},{"id":1143,"issue_id":"bd-rth1","author":"Codex","text":"Completed TLS handoff/teardown hardening and verification.

Implemented:
- Fixed TLS probe-chain deletion correctness in  (tombstone semantics preserve lookup chains).
- Replaced shared fallback TSD with per-thread fallback () to prevent cross-thread leakage for unregistered threads.
- Added ABI integration tests in :
  - 
  - 
  - shared guard + native-threading force helper for deterministic setup.

Verification evidence:
- Diff in /data/projects/frankenlibc/crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/thread.rs:301:
 #[cfg(target_arch = "x86_64")]
 #[allow(unsafe_code)]
 fn allocate_thread_stack(stack_size: usize) -> Result<(usize, usize, usize), i32> {
[31m-    let total_size = GUARD_PAGE_SIZE.checked_add(stack_size).ok_or(crate::errno::ENOMEM)?;
(B[m[32m+    let total_size = GUARD_PAGE_SIZE
(B[m[32m+        .checked_add(stack_size)
(B[m[32m+        .ok_or(crate::errno::ENOMEM)?;
(B[m 
     // Allocate the full region as read+write.
     // SAFETY: anonymous mmap with no fd, valid parameters. ✅
-  ✅
-  ✅
-

Note: full workspace test sweeps can still hit long-running/flaky hangs in pthread lifecycle/TSD binaries under concurrent workspace load; isolated bead-scope tests above pass consistently.","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:12:14Z"},{"id":1144,"issue_id":"bd-rth1","author":"Codex","text":"Completion summary (clean):

- Fixed TLS probe-chain deletion correctness in `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/tls.rs` (tombstone semantics preserve collision-chain lookups).
- Replaced shared fallback TSD with per-thread fallback (`thread_local!`) to prevent cross-thread leakage for unregistered threads.
- Added ABI TSD integration coverage in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_tsd_test.rs`:
  - `tsd_isolated_across_concurrent_threads`
  - `tsd_teardown_keeps_main_thread_clean`
  - serialized native-threading setup helper.

Verification:
- `cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-rth1 cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-rth1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-rth1 cargo test -p frankenlibc-core tls::tests -- --nocapture` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-rth1 cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_tsd_test tsd_isolated_across_concurrent_threads -- --nocapture` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-rth1 cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_tsd_test tsd_teardown_keeps_main_thread_clean -- --nocapture` ✅

Operational note:
- Full workspace test sweeps can still exhibit intermittent long-running pthread test binaries under heavy concurrent workspace load. Bead-scope TLS tests above pass consistently.

 {"id":"bd-s170","title":"Smoke-fuzz proptests for resolver parsers","description":"Archetype 1 (Crash Detector) via proptest for 4 resolver parsers that consume arbitrary bytes: parse_hosts_line, parse_services_line, ResolverConfig::parse, encode_domain_name. Each parser must never panic, must terminate on any byte sequence, and must produce outputs bounded relative to the input size (no infinite expansion). One parser per commit. Oracle: crash-free + size-bounded output.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T20:16:44.821539800Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T20:23:12.951422556Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T20:23:12.949455593Z","close_reason":"Added 13 smoke-fuzz proptests across 4 commits covering all 4 resolver parsers: 899230c (parse_hosts_line 3 tests), db6c4a4 (parse_services_line 3 tests), 96fae93 (ResolverConfig::parse 3 tests), 547c70e (encode_domain_name 4 tests). Each target has: (a) never-panics on arbitrary bytes, (b) structural invariant check, (c) where applicable, biased structured-alphabet fuzz to reach deep parser paths. ~6500 case evaluations per run.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-s1ew","title":"string: execute wide_memory.json fixture family through harness matrix","description":"Concrete gap: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/wide_memory_conformance_test.rs validates schema and in-process execution only. Add isolated harness subprocess execution for tests/conformance/fixtures/wide_memory.json, assert expected_output and host parity in strict/hardened modes, and fix any matrix-path mismatches that appear.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:12:06.310149587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T11:55:40.938509735Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T11:55:40.936301452Z","close_reason":"Executed wide_memory.json through the isolated harness conformance matrix while preserving the existing UB-tolerant host-parity exception; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-s39o","title":"EPIC: Expand LD_PRELOAD program compatibility beyond 61/61 core suite","description":"FrankenLibC currently passes 61/61 programs in the core LD_PRELOAD test suite PLUS 12+ complex programs (Python, Perl, Node.js, SSH, OpenSSL, Bash, Zsh, Vim, less, nano, curl, make, tar, gzip).

GOAL: Systematically expand compatibility to cover more real-world workloads. The next targets should be programs that exercise features we haven't fully tested:
- Database clients (psql, mysql, sqlite3)
- Build tools (cmake, ninja, gcc, rustc)
- Network tools (wget, dig, nslookup)
- Compression (bzip2, xz, zstd)
- Package managers (apt, dpkg)
- Editors/pagers (emacs, man)

APPROACH: Test each program, capture failure backtraces, fix root causes. Each fix typically benefits many programs.

ALREADY WORKING (verified):
61 core: cat ls echo wc head tail sort grep sed awk date env id uname whoami hostname pwd basename dirname mkdir rmdir touch rm cp mv ln chmod stat file dd tr cut paste fold uniq comm tee xargs yes seq expr test true false printf readlink realpath nproc getconf stty tty logname uptime arch git df du free kill sleep nice

 {"id":"bd-vfl","title":"Docs: align README/FEATURE_PARITY with support taxonomy (no overclaim)","description":"Critique mapping: #2 + bottom-line.

Deliverables:
- Update README to include an explicit truth table:
  - what this is today (interposition vs replacement)
  - which subsystems are still call-through
  - which are stubs
- Update FEATURE_PARITY.md to reflect the same classification.

Acceptance:
- No claim of full replacement unless supported by the matrix + E2E harness.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:37:46.293544458Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:53:21.767467115Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T05:53:21.767444111Z","close_reason":"Aligned README/FEATURE_PARITY to support taxonomy snapshot; removed full-replacement overclaims; added hybrid interposition truth table","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","docs"]}

 {"id":"bd-vj3","title":"Perf: Per-kernel microbench suite (criterion groups + thresholds)","description":"Add a criterion microbench suite that measures *per-kernel* costs, not just end-to-end decide().

## Why
End-to-end `decide()` can hide regressions. Per-kernel benches let us pin blame when a new kernel or refactor shifts cost.

## Bench Design
- Criterion group per kernel (or per family of kernels).
- Measure:
  - `decide` contributions (hot modules)
  - cadence update costs (epoch modules)
- Report ns/op and p95/p99 where applicable.

## Dependency
- Must reuse the baseline harness from `bd-d5l` and regression thresholds from `bd-242`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new kernel cannot land without adding/adjusting its microbench.
- Thresholds catch >X% regressions (configurable) in CI.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightMoose","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:03:30.833286241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:48.417613055Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:39.911611946Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-242","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-d5l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-pt6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1147,"issue_id":"bd-vj3","author":"BrightMoose","text":"Added per-kernel microbench binary crates/glibc-rs-bench/benches/runtime_math_kernels_bench.rs (machine-readable RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines) and wired it in crates/glibc-rs-bench/Cargo.toml.

Also hardened scripts/perf_gate.sh for dev environments:
- skip under extreme system load (GLIBC_RUST_PERF_SKIP_OVERLOADED=1, GLIBC_RUST_PERF_MAX_LOAD_FACTOR=0.85)
- optional kernel-suite run flag (GLIBC_RUST_PERF_ENABLE_KERNEL_SUITE=1) collecting RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines.

Baseline comparisons for kernel-suite metrics not yet enforced (needs baseline schema + stable host load to record p50s).","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:32:28Z"},{"id":1148,"issue_id":"bd-vj3","author":"CobaltCompass","text":"Verified complete by CobaltCompass. BrightMoose delivered per-kernel bench with 6 criterion groups: risk_upper_bound_ppm, bandit_select_profile, barrier_admissible, pareto_recommend_profile, design_choose_plan, approachability_observe/summary. Machine-readable RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines for CI gating. Wired into Cargo.toml and perf_gate.sh (GLIBC_RUST_PERF_ENABLE_KERNEL_SUITE=1). Note: per-kernel baseline enforcement deferred to stable host load recording.","created_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:48Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-vq1g","title":"D.1: (SUPERSEDED) — folded into parent bd-9dq5","description":"## Goal
Implement the request-tracking infrastructure for async DNS and the gai_error/gai_cancel functions.

## Design

### Request Registry
A global mutex-protected HashMap<usize, AsyncDnsRequest> maps gaicb pointer addresses to request state. The gaicb struct (defined by POSIX) contains:
- ar_name: hostname to resolve
- ar_service: service name/port
- ar_request: hints (addrinfo struct)
- ar_result: output addrinfo list (written on completion)

### gai_error(req: *mut gaicb) -> c_int
1. Look up req address in registry
2. If not found: return EAI_SYSTEM (unknown request)
3. Return state: EAI_INPROGRESS (still running), 0 (completed successfully), EAI_CANCELLED, or the error code from getaddrinfo

### gai_cancel(req: *mut gaicb) -> c_int
1. Look up req in registry
2. If not found: return EAI_NOCANCEL
3. If already completed: return EAI_ALLDONE
4. If in progress: set CANCELLED flag, return EAI_CANCELLED (or EAI_NOTCANCELED if thread already started resolution)

### State Constants
- EAI_INPROGRESS = -100 (glibc value)
- EAI_CANCELLED = -101
- EAI_ALLDONE = -103
- EAI_NOTCANCELED = -102

## Implementation
File: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs (near existing gai_* functions)
- Add AsyncDnsRequest struct
- Add static ASYNC_DNS_REGISTRY: LazyLock<Mutex<HashMap<usize, AsyncDnsRequest>>>
- Replace gai_error body
- Replace gai_cancel body

## Testing
- gai_error on unknown request returns EAI_SYSTEM
- gai_cancel on unknown request returns EAI_NOCANCEL
- Register a fake completed request, gai_error returns 0
- Register a fake in-progress request, gai_cancel returns EAI_CANCELLED

## Acceptance Criteria
State machine is correct. Registry tracks requests. Tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:44.987634072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:38.338086567Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:38.337881268Z","close_reason":"Superseded: Track D redesigned as synchronous-only. No request registry or futex wait needed. All logic fits in parent bd-9dq5.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","resolv","stub-elimination"]}
